text:
At first, I thought that was your boy sitting there, Brother Woods. On the end there, where the lady was writing, but it wasn't. When I opened my eyes, the Angel of the Lord was hanging there. I thought it was David sitting there. But it isn't David, I don't think. You're not David Woods, are you? You sure look like a twin brother to him, sitting right here. That's not David Woods.
I just seen something done. Amen. Oh, how marvelous. The Lord's ways are past finding out, aren't they? Amen. "If thou canst believe," said, "all things are possible."
All right, we're going to open the Word. I believe I'll just keep that to myself, let the Lord reveal it, as He sees need.
text_no:
Først trodde jeg det var din gutt, Bror Woods, som satt der. Ved enden, hvor damen skrev, men det var det ikke. Da jeg åpnet øynene, så jeg at Herrens Engel var der. Jeg trodde det var David som satt der, men det er ikke David. Du er ikke David Woods, er du? Du ser virkelig ut som tvillingbroren hans, sittende her. Men det er ikke David Woods.
Jeg så nettopp noe skje. Amen. Å, hvor vidunderlig. Herrens veier er uransakelige, er de ikke? Amen. "Om du kan tro," sa Han, "alt er mulig."
Vel, vi skal åpne Ordet. Jeg tror jeg vil beholde dette for meg selv og la Herren åpenbare det når Han ser det nødvendig.

text:
Over in the book of Numbers, the 20th chapter, now, quickly. And we'll try to take about twenty minutes for the sermon, if the Lord willing. And now, you be ready. I don't know what's going to happen now. The Lord Jesus just might do anything for us. Do you love Him? Say "Amen." Now, the word amen means "so be it," see. And, now, we really love Him.
And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
Take the rod, and gather … the assembly together, thou, and Aaron thy brother, and speak … to the rock … and it shall bring forth its waters, and thou shall bring forth water unto them, water out of the rock, and thou shalt give the congregation and their beasts water.
And Moses took the rod from before the LORD, as he commanded him.
Now, I want to speak just a few moments on this great servant, Moses.
text_no:
La oss raskt gå over til 4. Mosebok, kapittel 20. Vi skal prøve å bruke omtrent tyve minutter på prekenen, hvis Herren vil. Vær klare nå. Jeg vet ikke hva som kommer til å skje. Herren Jesus kan gjøre hva som helst for oss. Elsker dere Ham? Si "Amen." Ordet amen betyr "så være det." Vi elsker virkelig Ham.
Og HERREN talte til Moses og sa:
Ta staven, og samle menigheten, du og Aron, din bror, og tal til klippen, så den gir vann. Du skal hente vann til dem fra klippen, og gi både menigheten og deres dyr vann.
Og Moses tok staven fra HERRENs åsyn, som Han hadde befalt ham.
Nå vil jeg snakke noen få øyeblikk om denne store tjeneren, Moses.

text:
You feeling better, sister, that was sitting there writing? The little lady sit… Feel lots better now, don't you? You're healed, lady. That little heart trouble and things you've been bothered with, and kidney trouble, that's also you had varicose veins that had been bothering you. Isn't that right? If that's right, wave your hand. You were healed just then. Amen. Praise the Lord.
I looked around at her, and I seen her awhile ago, she looked so dreary, and I happened to look back, and I seen the Angel standing there. That looked so much like David. You ought to meet Mr. Woods, over here. You look like his boy, enough to be his double. I looked back, and I thought, Maybe that's David, 'cause I knowed David loved me and believes in me.
And I looked down, and it was hanging over that little lady. And then I seen what her trouble was. All at once, I looked back, and she was just smiling. I thought, I might as well tell her, let her know it's over, so she can… Amen. She was taking down my notes, or something there.
All right. Oh, isn't He wonderful? That's just the way it works. That's just the way it does. Just … you just can't beat Him. He's just here to help us.
text_no:
Føler du deg bedre nå, søster, som satt der og skrev? Den lille damen… Føler deg mye bedre nå, ikke sant? Du er helbredet. Det lille hjerteproblemet og de andre plagene du har hatt, inkludert nyreproblemer og åreknuter, har forsvunnet. Er ikke det riktig? Hvis det er riktig, vink med hånden. Du ble helbredet akkurat da. Amen. Pris Herren.
Jeg så på henne, og tidligere virket hun så trist. Så vendte jeg blikket tilbake og så Engelen stå der. Hun minnet meg så mye om David. Du burde møte Bror Woods her. Hun ligner sønnen hans, som om de var dobbeltgjengere. Jeg så tilbake og innså at det hang over den lille damen. Da så jeg hva hennes problemer var. Alt på en gang, så jeg henne smile. Jeg tenkte at jeg kunne like gjerne fortelle henne at det er over, så hun vet det. Amen. Hun noterte det jeg sa, eller noe lignende.
All right. Å, er ikke Han fantastisk? Slik fungerer det. Slik virker Han. Du kan ikke slå Ham. Han er her for å hjelpe oss.

text:
Now, the Lord said … told Moses to get the rod. Now, we want to speak on the rod, and the rod pertaining to judgments. I just love to talk about Moses. He was the priest, king, and law-giver; everything a type of the Lord Jesus Christ, Moses was.
He was born in the world, a fair child. In other words, he was born a prophet. They wasn't afraid of the king, when they seen God… What kind of a thing happened when Moses was born… It does not record, but something happened when Moses was born, 'cause his parents seen it, and know that … they didn't care what the king said, they knowed their son was going to be safe; they didn't fear.
text_no:
Nå sa Herren til Moses at han skulle ta staven. Vi skal nå snakke om staven og dens betydning for dommer. Jeg elsker å snakke om Moses. Han var prest, konge og lovgiver; alt som Moses var, er en type på Herren Jesus Kristus.
Moses ble født som et rettferdig barn. Med andre ord, han ble født som en profet. De fryktet ikke kongen da de så Gud... Hva som skjedde da Moses ble født, er ikke nedskrevet, men noe må ha skjedd fordi foreldrene hans så det. De brydde seg ikke om hva kongen sa; de visste at sønnen deres ville være trygg. De fryktet ikke.

text:
So, he was pulled up out of the bulrushes, and was raised right with … just in every way. Went into the wilderness, and led the children of Israel; a law-giver, and a priest, and he was everything that Christ was. In type, he was, and Christ was the antetype.
Now, if we'll notice, Moses, when he became the age of about forty, he refused to be called Pharaoh's daughter… Moses' life runs in a cycle, of three cycles; first, forty; next, eighty; then a hundred and twenty, when he died. Just a perfect…
I could go through the Scripture, and show that … of the dispensations of grace, of water, blood, and spirit; of justification, sanctification, the baptism of the Holy Ghost; the first, second, and third coming of Christ. Everything you want to type, lays right smack … and everything in the Scripture will roll right in to them letters, just like that.
text_no:
Han ble løftet opp fra sivet og vokste opp på alle måter som en egypter. Han gikk ut i ørkenen og ledet Israels barn; en lovgiver og en prest, og han var alt det Kristus var. I type var han det, og Kristus var antitypen.
Nå, hvis vi ser på Moses, når han ble rundt førti år, nektet han å bli kalt faraos datters sønn. Moses' liv går i en syklus på tre: først førti år, deretter åtti, og til slutt hundre og tjue år, da han døde. En perfekt...
Jeg kunne gå gjennom Skriften og vise dispensasjonene av nåde, vann, blod og Ånd; rettferdiggjørelse, helliggjørelse og dåpen av Den Hellige Ånd; Kristi første, andre og tredje komme. Alt du ønsker å type, faller nøyaktig på plass, og alt i Skriften passer sømløst inn i disse malene.

text:
Now, and Moses, when he … the first forty years he was taught… Who do you think Moses' teacher was? His mother. He couldn't have had any better, 'cause she was hired as a tutor, to raise her own child. I tell you, God certainly pulled one over on Satan there, didn't He? He sure did. And Moses, knowing now, from his mother, that he was raised up … her a spiritual woman.
Little is said about her, but, brother, I want to be in glory when she receives her crown. Yes, sir, to see what takes place. How she taught that little fellow right in the midst of the enemy, right there, in the furnace of the heat of it, and told him that he would be the one that would deliver God's children out of that place.
text_no:
Moses fikk sin utdannelse de første førti årene … Hvem tror du underviste ham? Hans mor. Han kunne ikke fått noen bedre lærer, for hun ble ansatt som veileder for å oppdra sitt eget barn. Gud lurte virkelig Satan der, gjorde Han ikke? Det gjorde Han absolutt. Og Moses visste nå, fra sin mor, at han hadde blitt oppdratt av en åndelig kvinne.
Det sies lite om henne, men bror, jeg ønsker å være i herligheten når hun får sin krone. Ja, det vil jeg, for å se hva som skjer. Hvordan hun underviste den lille gutten midt blant fiendene, midt i varmen fra ildovnen, og fortalte ham at han ville være den som skulle frigjøre Guds barn fra det stedet.

text:
Moses knowing then, as a scholar, "I guess I'll just go out and set the thing in order," he said. So, he was forty years old, so he thought, I'm getting old enough to do it. So he took the thing over in his own hand, and failed. And every time that we take the matter in our hands, we're going to fail. You just go as the Lord leads, and what the Lord says do. Then you do that, and you'll never fail. You can't fail, because you're following the leading of the Lord.
If Moses would've only waited just a little while, and let things get ripe, and let the thing get … and the season. You can't plant corn now, and go out and gather it in an hour. You've got to let it lay there, and the seed die, and rot away, and new life come up, in spring, in the corn.
That's the way we do now. We're putting the seed in the hearts of the people, that when this great time of reaping comes, (see what I mean?) it'll be materialized, and be a great thing happen.
text_no:
Moses, som visste dette som en lærd, tenkte: "Jeg antar jeg bare får ordne opp i dette," sa han. Siden han var førti år gammel, mente han at han var gammel nok til å gjøre det. Så han tok saken i sine egne hender, og mislyktes. Hver gang vi tar saken i egne hender, vil vi mislykkes. Følg Herrens ledelse, og gjør det Herren sier. Da vil du aldri mislykkes. Du kan ikke mislykkes når du følger Herrens ledelse.
Hadde Moses bare ventet litt til, latt tingene bli modne og sesongen komme... Du kan ikke plante mais nå og høste den en time senere. Du må la den ligge der, la frøet dø og råtne bort, og la nytt liv spire om våren fra maisen.
Slik gjør vi det nå også. Vi planter såkorn i menneskers hjerter, slik at når denne store innhøstningstiden kommer, vil det materialisere seg og noe stort vil skje.

text: Now, Moses, after taking the situation in his own hand, found out that he was a total failure, and run from the presence of Pharaoh, and the presence of God, and was a stranger for forty years. Way back, and he married an Ethiopian girl. And so, she had a lot of temper. And Moses had a lot of temper. That was his trouble. And I just imagine things wasn't so peaceful, back there, on the back of the desert, at times. But God gave it to him.
text_no: Moses, etter å ha tatt situasjonen i egne hender, oppdaget at han var en total fiasko. Han rømte både fra Faraos nærvær og Guds nærvær, og var en fremmed i førti år. Han giftet seg med en etiopisk jente som hadde et hett temperament, noe Moses også hadde. Det var hans problem. Jeg kan bare forestille meg at det ikke alltid var fredelig der bak i ørkenen. Men Gud tillot det.

text:
Now, if you think you married a woman has a little temper, maybe God is trying to tame you down a little, (you see?) so, and vice-versa.
So, Zipporah was a, kind of high-tempered. She proved it when she cut the foreskin from her son and throwed it before Moses, said, "Thou art a bloody husband to me." My! I imagine things wasn't too peaceful, at times. Anyhow back there, God was… What was He doing? Schooling into the boy's mind … or, the man's mind, what the program of God was.
Moses was back there waiting, and now there he was. What did he do? He went to work for his father in law, Jethro, and he was herding his sheep, out in the desert place. There is a perfect picture here before us tonight, of a believer out of fellowship with God.
text_no:
Hvis du mener at du giftet deg med en kvinne med litt temperament, er det kanskje Gud som prøver å dempe deg litt, (du forstår?) og omvendt.
Zipporah hadde et temperament. Hun viste det da hun kuttet forhuden fra sin sønn og kastet den foran Moses og sa: "Du er en blodig ektemann for meg." Jeg kan forestille meg at det ikke alltid var så fredelig. Uansett, hva gjorde Gud tilbake der? Han skolerte gutten... eller mannens sinn, om hva Guds program var.
Moses ventet der, og hva gjorde han? Han begynte å arbeide for sin svigerfar, Jetro, og gjetet hans sauer ute i ørkenen. Dette er et perfekt bilde på en troende som er uten fellesskap med Gud.

text:
There he was, no fellowship, not one thing in that forty years was spoke that God did for him, or any way, any supernatural. He was out of fellowship. And when the Church gets out of fellowship with God, miracles cease; signs and wonders cease; revivals cease. God just moves right out, when you get out of fellowship.
The thing to do, is keep that, (what I was trying to say a while a go) the love of God in your heart. Keep fellowship with Him, and he will add these other things, just as we mature. Don't you think so?
And so, Moses, out of fellowship, no fellowship--out there in a strange country, amongst strangers, not his own people. She was… The people there were Ethiopians, and he was a Jew. And there, completely out of reach of God, seemingly, and God all the time knowing that no matter how much of a mess that man makes out of the program of God, God is going to straighten it out. That's right.
text_no:
Der var han, uten fellesskap. I løpet av de førtifem årene ble det ikke sagt én ting om hva Gud gjorde for ham, eller på noen måte, noe overnaturlig. Han var ute av fellesskap. Når Menigheten mister fellesskapet med Gud, opphører mirakler, tegn og undere; vekkelsene stanser. Gud trekker seg bort når du går ut av fellesskap.
Det viktigste er å bevare Guds kjærlighet i ditt hjerte, som jeg prøvde å si for en stund siden. Hold fellesskap med Ham, og Han vil tilføre disse andre tingene etter hvert som vi modnes. Er du ikke enig?
Så, Moses, uten fellesskap, helt alene i et fremmed land, blant fremmede, ikke sitt eget folk. Folket der var etiopiere, og han var jøde. Helt utenfor rekkevidde av Gud, tilsynelatende, men Gud visste hele tiden at uansett hvor mye et menneske roter til Guds plan, vil Gud rette det opp. Det er riktig.

text:
So, just know that there's going to be a church appear before God, without spot, without blemish, without a wrinkle. God is going to do it. And if I fail to preach the truth, and Brother Joseph fails to preach the truth, and you brethren out there fail to preach the truth, "God is able of these stones to rise children unto Abraham." Somebody is going to preach and contend for the faith that was once delivered to the saints. Somebody is going to pull the church on. That's right.
So, no matter how much the picture was marred, it took God forty years to rectify what Moses done, in about three days, just get that picture straightened out again. He just took Moses and set him out here, and gave him a high-tempered wife to kind of get him straightened out, and God went on down here fixing the picture around again, till He got it straightened out. But He was determined that He was going to do it, and He ordained Moses to do so. Oh, I just love that.
text_no:
Vit at det skal stå fram en menighet for Gud, uten flekk, lyte eller rynke. Gud vil gjøre dette. Og hvis jeg feiler i å forkynne sannheten, og Bror Joseph feiler i å forkynne sannheten, og dere brødre der ute feiler i å forkynne sannheten, "Gud er i stand til å reise barn til Abraham av disse steinene." Noen kommer til å forkynne og kjempe for troen som en gang ble overlevert til de hellige. Noen vil føre menigheten fremover. Det er riktig.
Uansett hvor mye bildet ble ødelagt, tok det Gud førti år å rette opp det Moses gjorde på omtrent tre dager for å få bildet i orden igjen. Han tok bare Moses og plasserte ham her, og ga ham en hissig kone for å få ham til å gå på riktig vei, og Gud gikk videre med å ordne bildet til Han fikk det rett. Men Han var bestemt på at Han skulle gjøre det, og Han utvalgte Moses til dette. Å, jeg elsker det.

text:
What God has determined is going to happen. Oh, don't it give you a wonderful feeling? What God has determined, it has to be. That's right. He's going to do it, anyhow.
So, what a time we can see, is God fixing His picture back. And one day, while Moses, out of fellowship with God, perhaps thought, Well, the vision of ever delivering the children of Israel is all past and gone. I guess I'll live and die here in the desert, and that'll be all there is to it. Why was I ever born in the world, or perhaps walking around there. And he had a stick in his hand. And he was walking along.
And God appeared in the form of an Angel, in a burning bush. And Moses seen it. I imagine he said, "Now way back, out here, where there's no more herders but myself, I wonder what that bush is on fire about. Has somebody slipped in here, and set the bush afire?" He watched it, and he watched it, it didn't… God's got a way of attracting your attention some way, hasn't He?
text_no:
Det Gud har bestemt, skal skje. Gir ikke det en vidunderlig følelse? Det Gud har bestemt, det må skje. Det er riktig. Han vil utføre det uansett.
Tenk på hvilken tid vi kan se, når Gud gjenoppretter sitt bilde. En dag, mens Moses, ute av fellesskap med Gud, kanskje tenkte: "Nå er visjonen om å befri Israels barn helt forbi og glemt. Jeg antar at jeg vil leve og dø her i ørkenen, og det vil være alt. Hvorfor ble jeg noensinne født, eller vandrende rundt her?" Med en stokk i hånden gikk han rundt.
Da viste Gud seg i form av en Engel i en brennende busk. Moses så det. Jeg forestiller meg at han sa: "Langt her ute, hvor det ikke er flere gjetere enn meg, hva er det som gjør at denne busken brenner? Har noen sneket seg hit og tent på busken?" Han fulgte med, og han fulgte med, men busken brant ikke opp… Gud har en måte å fange oppmerksomheten din på, har Han ikke?

text:
He got attention of Jairus, one day, or Little Jairus, when he wouldn't believe in divine healing. He believed it; and he wanted Jairus' heart, so He just… Only thing He could do, was strike his daughter and let her die, so that Jesus come over, and raise her up again, just to really get his attention. You see? God does that. Sometimes throws you on your back, sometimes, sick, so He can make you look up, that you'll realize.
Like the old shepherd story of … had a sheep, they broke its leg. And he said, "How did the sheep break its leg?"
He said, "I broke its leg."
Said, "Why, you're a cruel shepherd, to break your own sheep's leg."
text_no:
En dag fanget Han oppmerksomheten til Jairus, også kalt Lille Jairus, som ikke ville tro på guddommelig helbredelse. Han trodde, men han ville ha Jairus' hjerte, så Han gjorde det eneste Han kunne: Han lot datteren bli syk og dø, slik at Jesus kunne komme og vekke henne til live. På den måten fikk Han virkelig Jairus' oppmerksomhet. Gud gjør det noen ganger. Han kan legge deg ned eller gjøre deg syk for at du skal se opp og innse Hans kraft.
Dette kan sammenlignes med den gamle hyrdehistorien om en sau som brakk beinet sitt. Noen spurte, "Hvordan brakk sauen sitt bein?" Hyrden svarte, "Jeg brakk beinet." Personen svarte, "Hvorfor, du er en grusom hyrde som bryter beinet på din egen sau."

text:
Said, "No." Said, "She wouldn't mind me." And said, "Then I had to break her leg, in order that I could pack her around, and baby her a little bit, and give her some special food, so that she would love me and follow me."
That's the way God has to do sometimes. Kind of break us down, once in a while, to give us a little extra food, you know, to kind of love us a little bit. Do you know that's exactly why I'm a Gospel preacher, tonight? is because it come by a spell of sickness, when the doctor said I couldn't live. That's exactly right. It changed me, shook me.
I hated the thoughts of a preacher. And one time, while I had the bantam weight championship, a fellow said, "Say!" (Had a blue coat on). Said, "You look like one of these little boxed-backed, Baptist preachers."
text_no:
Han sa, "Nei." Han sa, "Hun ville ikke høre på meg." Og sa, "Derfor måtte jeg brekke beinet hennes, slik at jeg kunne bære henne rundt, pleie henne litt og gi henne spesialmat, så hun ville elske meg og følge meg."
Det er slik Gud noen ganger må gjøre det. Han må bryte oss ned av og til, for å gi oss litt ekstra næring og kjærlighet. Vet du, det er akkurat derfor jeg er en forkynner av Evangeliet i kveld? Det skjedde gjennom en sykdomsperiode da legen sa at jeg ikke kunne leve. Det er helt riktig. Det forandret meg, rystet meg.
Jeg hatet tanken på å være en forkynner. Og en gang, mens jeg hadde bantamvektmestertittelen, sa en fyr, "Si!" (Han hadde en blå frakk på). Han sa, "Du ser ut som en av disse små, stive, baptistforkynnerne."

text:
And I said, "Look fellow, you smile when you say that." Say, "I was right then ready to go fight with my partner. No preacher about me." And you see what God did? If He had to lay me on my back, when the doctors says, "It's all over."
And then He come around and give me a little special treatment, kind of poured in the oil, you know, and said, "Now, I love you."
I said, "Yes, Lord. I love you, too." So, so then we became friends. That's how Jesus does sometimes, has to pour in a little of the balm of Gilead, you know, to kind of soothe things over, to show you He loves you, He heals you, and wants you to be well, and love Him, and serve Him, believe in Him. Isn't He wonderful?
Then, that's the way He was doing Moses, back there. And Moses stepped aside, (attracted his attention) and he looked back over there. He thought, Well, now, Moses, I guess you think now that…
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Hør her, kompis, smil når du sier det." Jeg var virkelig klar for å slåss med partneren min. Det var ingen forkynner i meg. Og ser du hva Gud gjorde? Han måtte legge meg på ryggen når legene sa, "Det er over."
Så kom Han og ga meg litt spesiell behandling, som å helle olje i sårene mine, og sa, "Nå, Jeg elsker deg." Jeg svarte, "Ja, Herre. Jeg elsker Deg også." Så ble vi venner. Slik gjør Jesus noen ganger; Han må helle i litt av balsam fra Gilead for å lindre ting, vise at Han elsker deg, helbreder deg, og ønsker at du skal være frisk, elske Ham, og tjene Ham, tro på Ham. Er Han ikke vidunderlig?
Slik holdt Han på med Moses, der borte. Moses stoppet opp (noe fanget oppmerksomheten hans) og kikket tilbake. Han tenkte, "Vel, Moses, nå tror jeg du..."

text:
I imagine the Lord seen poor, old Moses going crippling along there, with his long, white beard and hair.
"Well, years ago, I had a vision, and I thought the Lord would surely deliver the children of Israel, but God has done turned his back on me now. I'm back over here battling it out. So I guess I'll just have to stay this way."
And I imagine God said, "Poor fellow. I just had to do that to let you realize … but I am going to show you now that I'm with you."
So, He just attracted his attention. Well, He wanted to get an audience with Moses. You know, God sometime has to get you sick, and in the hospital, where the doctor says you ain't going to live, to get an audience with you. You'll call for it, don't you worry. You just wait till the doctor says… Oh, you say, "I don't believe in divine healing, no such thing. That thing's nonsense." Let the doctor say, "All hopes is gone." God'll get you … an audience with you. He has a way of doing things. He does it in His own way. Hallelujah! My, that just makes me feel like being a real shouting Methodist.
text_no:
Jeg forestiller meg at Herren så den fattige, gamle Moses som haltet langs med sitt lange, hvite skjegg og hår.
"Vel, for mange år siden hadde jeg en visjon, og jeg trodde Herren sikkert ville befri Israels barn, men Gud har nå vendt Seg bort fra meg. Nå er jeg tilbake her og kjemper. Så jeg antar at jeg bare må forbli slik."
Og jeg forestiller meg at Gud sa, "Stakkars fyr. Jeg måtte bare gjøre det for at du skulle innse … men nå skal Jeg vise deg at Jeg er med deg."
Så, Han tiltok seg Moses' oppmerksomhet. Gud ønsket å få en samtale med Moses. Noen ganger må Gud få deg syk og på sykehuset, der legen sier at du ikke kommer til å overleve, for å få en samtale med deg. Du vil kalle på Ham, ikke bekymre deg. Bare vent til legen sier … Å, du sier, "Jeg tror ikke på guddommelig helbredelse, det er bare nonsens." La legen si, "Alle håp er ute." Gud vil få til en samtale med deg. Han har sin egen måte å gjøre ting på. Han gjør det på Sin egen måte. Halleluja! Åh, det får meg til å føle meg som en ekte jubelmetodist.

text:
Look. Because it's God does it. You see? He just does it in His own way. He just… He's got a way of doing things, hasn't He? And I'm so glad that He has. Amen.
So, we see now, that he got an audience with Moses, but saying, now, this would be a very odd, odd sight. You know God does things kindly odd sometimes, to get the audience. That's right.
Somebody came to me, and said, "Hey, they say they got a holy-roller, down here, going to preach tonight. I believe I'll just go down, and see what it's all about."
The Milltown Baptist pastor, that's in my place at the Milltown Baptist Church, tonight, William Hall, that fellow came over, 'cause he had it put in the paper. Said, "Say, you ought to come hear this fellow preach. He's a little Billy Sunday."
text_no:
Se. Fordi det er Gud som gjør det. Forstår du? Han gjør det på Sin egen måte. Han har en egen måte å gjøre ting på, har Han ikke? Og jeg er så glad for at Han har det. Amen.
Så, vi ser nå at han fikk en samtale med Moses, men dette ville være et veldig merkelig syn. Du vet, Gud gjør ting litt merkelig noen ganger, for å få folks oppmerksomhet. Det stemmer.
Noen kom til meg og sa: "Hei, de sier at de har en hellig-ruller her nede som skal forkynne i kveld. Jeg tror jeg skal dra ned og se hva det handler om."
William Hall, pastoren ved Milltown Baptist kirke, som er på min plass i kveld, sa: "Du burde komme og høre denne fyren forkynne. Han er en liten Billy Sunday."

text:
And here he came over, oh, was he rank. His hair hanging down in his face, and had one tooth out, and a big corn-cob pipe, and he rapped it on the side of the church, like that, when he knocked it out. Walked in, and said, "Where is that little Billy Sunday? I want to hear him."
The deacon brothers said, "Now, sit down, Mr. Hall." He was a rough customer.
And then he said, "Maybe I'd better get up a little closer. I want to see what Billy Sunday looked like," he said. Just making fun. You see? And that night, he got saved. Amen. And he's the pastor, there in that same church, tonight.
text_no:
Og der kom han, å, så rufsete han var. Håret hang ned i ansiktet, og han hadde en tann som manglet, samt en stor maispiper med seg. Han slo pipen mot siden av menighetsbygget for å tømme den. Så gikk han inn og sa: "Hvor er den lille Billy Sunday? Jeg vil høre ham."
Diakonene sa: "Sett deg ned, Mr. Hall." Han var en røff type.
Deretter sa han: "Kanskje jeg skal komme litt nærmere. Jeg vil se hvordan Billy Sunday ser ut," sa han hånlig. Men den kvelden ble han frelst. Amen. Og i kveld er han pastor i den samme menigheten.

text:
See, God has a way of attracting you, doesn't He? Just gets you aside one time.
So he said, "Now this'll look a little strange to Moses, something out of the ordinary. So, I'll just send this pillar of fire down there in that bush, and let it … 'cause it's going to lead him all through the wilderness, anyhow. And so, I'll just let it start burning, get Moses to come over here."
So, it attracted Moses' attention. So, he draws over a little closer, you know, and said, "Well, that looks strange. That thing don't burn up." When he got close enough, in speaking distance… Oh, I want to get there, don't you? In speaking distance, just get in speaking distance.
He said, "Moses."
He said, "Yes, Lord? Here I am."
"Take off your shoes now. You're on holy ground."
So, Moses unlatched his shoes, and walked up a little closer, said, "What is it, Lord?"
text_no:
Se, Gud har en måte å tiltrekke deg på, ikke sant? Han får deg til side en gang.
Han sa: "Nå vil dette se litt rart ut for Moses, noe utenom det vanlige. Så jeg sender denne ildsøylen ned i den busken, for den skal uansett lede ham gjennom ørkenen. Jeg lar den begynne å brenne, så Moses kommer hit."
Det fanget Moses' oppmerksomhet. Han nærmet seg litt mer og sa: "Vel, det ser rart ut. Den tingen brenner ikke opp." Da han kom nærme nok til å høre Guds stemme, å, jeg vil komme dit, vil ikke du? Komme til å høre Guds stemme.
Gud sa: "Moses."
Han svarte: "Ja, Herre? Her er jeg."
"Ta av deg skoene nå. Du står på hellig grunn."
Moses løsnet skoene og nærmet seg litt mer. Han sa: "Hva er det, Herre?"

text:
And He said, "I have heard… Now, Moses, I ain't saying one word to you, about what I've seen about you, but I've heard the groans of My people, and I have remembered my covenant with Abraham." Amen. His Word, in other words. "I remember what I promised Abraham."
The same promise He gave Abraham, He has gave to you, for you're the seed of Abraham. And He said, "I remember My promise with Abraham, and I've come down to deliver My children, take them back to the promised land, and I'm sending you down."
Moses said, "Oh, well, you see I can't speak very well." Said, "I'm not eloquent." And said, "I … I can't speak good." And said, "I'm slow of speech." He said, "I'm … I just can't do it." He said, "Well, ah, Aaron's on his road up," and so forth.
And so He said, "What you got in your hand, Moses?"
He said, "A stick." That's all he had, just an old stick. Picked it up out there on the desert, somewhere, where he'd punched the sheep through the gates, or where ever he went, get them along. Just an old stick he'd cut down, 'cause he said it was a stick.
text_no:
Og Herren sa: "Jeg har hørt... Nå, Moses, jeg sier ikke ett ord til deg om hva Jeg har sett hos deg, men Jeg har hørt sukkene fra Mitt folk, og Jeg har husket Min pakt med Abraham." Amen. Hans Ord, med andre ord. "Jeg husker hva Jeg lovet Abraham."
Det samme løftet som Han ga Abraham, har Han gitt dere, for dere er Abrahams ætt. Og Han sa: "Jeg husker Mitt løfte til Abraham, og Jeg har kommet ned for å befri Mine barn, føre dem tilbake til det lovede land, og Jeg sender deg ned."
Moses sa: "Vel, du ser, jeg kan ikke tale særlig godt. Jeg er ikke veltalende. Jeg ... jeg kan ikke snakke bra. Jeg er tung i tale." Han sa: "Jeg ... jeg bare kan ikke gjøre det." Herren svarte: "Aaron er på vei opp," og så videre.
Og Han sa: "Hva har du i hånden, Moses?"
Han svarte: "En stav." Det var alt han hadde, bare en gammel stav. Han hadde plukket den opp der i ørkenen et sted, brukt den til å geleide sauene gjennom portene, eller hvor enn han måtte gå. Bare en gammel stav han hadde kuttet ned, for han sa at det var en stav.

text:
He said, "Moses, throw it down." And when he threw the stick down, it turned into a serpent, and Moses fled. And He said, "Pick it up." And he took it by the tail, and it turned back to a stick again.
Moses, I guess could say, "I've seen strange sights today," so when he seen that stick turned to a snake. Now, what was that stick? That's what we're going to speak on. What was that stick?
It wasn't an ordinary stick anymore. For it was the judgment rod of Almighty God. What did it symbolize? The cross. And then, how would it symbolize the cross? Because, on the natural, it was wood. On the spiritual, it was a serpent. And how could the serpent represent Jesus?
The serpent, as it was a reptile, a snake, it represented sin already judged. For Satan had sinned, in the serpent, in the garden of Eden, which was not a reptile. He was the missing link between man and animal. And he stood there as a … or, walked upright, and was the most subtle of all the beasts--not reptiles--of the beasts of the field.
And when he was cursed, his legs went off of him, and on his belly he went. And that showed that he was already judged, when he became a reptile.
text_no:
Han sa: "Moses, kast det ned." Og da Moses kastet staven ned, ble den til en slange, og Moses flyktet. Så sa Han: "Ta den opp igjen." Da Moses tok den ved halen, ble den til en stav igjen.
Moses kunne nok ha sagt: "Jeg har sett merkelige ting i dag," etter å ha sett staven bli til en slange. Nå, hva var denne staven? Det er det vi skal snakke om. Hva var denne staven?
Den var ikke lenger en vanlig stav. Den var domsstaven til Den Allmektige Gud. Hva symboliserte den? Korset. Hvordan symboliserte den korset? Fordi, i det naturlige, var den av tre. I det åndelige, var den en slange. Og hvordan kunne slangen representere Jesus?
Slangen, som en reptil, en slange, representerte synd som allerede var dømt. For Satan hadde syndet, i slangen, i Edens hage, som ikke var en reptil. Han var den manglende koblingen mellom menneske og dyr. Han stod der, eller gikk oppreist, og var den mest listige av alle dyrene--ikke reptilene--av dyrene på marken.
Da han ble forbannet, mistet han bena, og på magen krøp han. Det viste at han allerede var dømt da han ble en reptil.

text:
And then Christ was sin, in the serpent, already judged, the Lamb slain from the foundation of the world. You see it?
Now, he picked up the stick, and the supernatural on the stick, was a serpent. Supernatural, it was a serpent in his hand. Natural, it was a stick in his hand; the cross plus Christ, the judgment.
Here he goes down to Egypt, as I many times said, "A one-man invasion." Had his wife, run and got his wife, and sat her on the mule, a child on each hip, and here he was going towards Egypt, going down to take over. Could you imagine a one-man invasion going to Egypt, to take over? But he did it, because God promised him he would do it.
text_no:
Kristus var allerede synden i slangen, dømt, og Lammet slaktet fra verdens grunnvoll ble lagt. Ser du det? 
Han tok opp staven, som overnaturlig ble til en slange i hånden hans. Naturlig var den en stav; det overnaturlige var dommen i form av en slange – korset sammen med Kristus. 
Han reiste til Egypt, som jeg ofte sier, "en enmanns invasjon." Han tok med seg sin kone, satte henne på muldyret med et barn på hver hofte, og dro mot Egypt for å overta. Kan du forestille deg en enmanns invasjon til Egypt for å overta? Men han gjorde det, fordi Gud hadde lovet ham at han skulle klare det.

text:
And while he was down there, when Moses went in, he waved this stick across the rivers, and they turned into blood. Then he waved them towards the skies, and the sun went dark. And everywhere he put that stick before him, it was judgment. Judgment went before the stick. Because the stick was the judgment rod. You get it?
Now, when he wanted fleas, he raised this stick toward the air, like that, and sprinkled some dirt, and as the dust blowed on, fleas came from everywhere. Judgments, divine judgment.
He brought fire out of the heaven, lightning upon the earth, great hailstones and everything, that killed the cattle, and killed the Egyptians and everything else; judgment. God's judgment before Moses.
text_no:
Mens han var der nede, da Moses gikk inn, svingte han staven over elvene, og de ble til blod. Deretter løftet han staven mot himmelen, og solen ble mørk. Overalt hvor han plasserte staven foran seg, kom dommen. Dommen gikk foran staven, for staven var domsstaven. Forstår dere?
Når han ønsket lopper, hevet han staven mot luften og drysset litt jord. Når støvet spredte seg, kom lopper fra alle kanter. Dommer, guddommelig dom.
Han brakte ild ned fra himmelen, lyn på jorden, store haglsteiner og alt som drepte buskapen og egypterne; dom. Guds dom før Moses.

text:
Now, as Moses went with the judgment stick before him, that same stick, tonight, to the church, is Jesus Christ. If those Egyptians could have ever got that little, simple stick out of Moses' hands, they'd have had him whipped. And when the devil could ever get the deity out of the name of Jesus Christ, he's got the church whipped.
But as the judgment stick went before Moses, to take the judgments … or, bring the judgment, so does the name of Jesus go before us, to take our judgment. Stands our judgment of sickness; stands our judgment of death; stands our judgment of trouble.
Take the name of Jesus with you,
As a shield from every care,
When temptations around you gather,
Breathe that holy name in prayer.
text_no:
Nå, slik Moses gikk med domsstaven foran seg, er den samme staven, i kveld, Jesus Kristus for Menigheten. Hvis egypterne noen gang hadde klart å ta den enkle staven ut av Moses' hender, ville de ha vunnet over ham. Og når djevelen klarer å fjerne guddommeligheten fra navnet Jesus Kristus, har han beseiret Menigheten.
Men slik som Moses bar domsstaven for å utføre dom, går Jesu navn foran oss for å ta vår dom. Han bærer vår dom over sykdom; Han bærer vår dom over døden; Han bærer vår dom over vanskeligheter.
Ta navnet Jesus med deg,
Som et skjold mot alle sorger,
Når fristelser samler seg rundt deg,
Pust det hellige navnet i bønn.

text:
When sickness begins to gather, breathe that name of Jesus. What is it? It's God's representative of judgment. Christ already judged for your sins; Christ already judged for your temptation; Christ already judged for your sickness. See what I mean? It was God's judgment rod. And as long as that rod waved on, it was judgment.
Moses, that night, after leading the children of Israel out, and there came Pharaoh's army, the pillar of fire went down and hung between Pharaoh and pursuing army, and Israel. It made light to Israel, and darkness to Pharaoh, and kept them apart. Amen. A go-between. Oh, I'm so glad today that He is our go-between.
text_no:
Når sykdom begynner å samle seg, pust ut navnet Jesus. Hva er det? Det er Guds representant for dom. Kristus er allerede dømt for dine synder, Kristus er allerede dømt for dine fristelser, Kristus er allerede dømt for din sykdom. Ser du hva jeg mener? Det var Guds domsstav. Og så lenge den staven ble viftet med, var det dom.
Moses, den natten etter å ha ledet Israels barn ut, og der kom faraos hær. Ildstøtten kom ned og hang mellom farao og hans forfølgende hær og Israel. Den ga lys til Israel og mørke til farao og holdt dem adskilt. Amen. En mellommann. Åh, jeg er så glad i dag at Han er vår mellommann.

text:
In sicknesses in between death and life, He's my go-between. He's the One that takes death in one hand, and life in the other one. He's that link that connects man to God; the go-between.
And He came down as a go-between. And He made light on this side for Israel to march on, and darkness to hold Pharaoh back. That same Angel of God is in the church tonight, giving light for the church to walk on, and darkness for those who reject it. We're children of the light. Amen.
How can you see when you walk in darkness? You can't see. You don't know where you're going. But if you're in the light, and Christ is the Light. And He's giving light to those believers, while He was giving darkness to the unbelievers. He was showing a way of a path to the believer, and a darkness and stumbling about to the unbeliever.
text_no:
I sykdommens grenseland mellom liv og død er Han vår mellommann. Han er Den som holder døden i én hånd og livet i den andre. Han er koblingen som forener mennesket med Gud; mellommannen.
Han kom ned som en mellommann og skapte lys på denne siden for at Israel kunne marsjere videre, mens Han holdt igjen Farao med mørke. Den samme Guds Engel er i menigheten i kveld, og gir lys til menigheten for å vandre i, mens de som avviser dette lyset, holdes tilbake i mørket. Vi er lysets barn. Amen.
Hvordan kan du se når du vandrer i mørket? Du kan ikke se. Du vet ikke hvor du går. Men hvis du er i lyset, og Kristus er Lyset, gir Han lys til troende, mens Han skaper mørke for de vantro. Han viser veien til de troende, mens de vantro famler i mørket og snubler.

text:
And so is it tonight. As we're marching towards the promised land, God's throwing light on the path of divine healing, speaking with tongues, shoutings and the glories of God, manifesting them to the children of the light, and throwing darkness back there, that they stumble, not knowing where they're going.
Remember that same stick, that hand that held that judgment rod, when he come down and there was God's path, running through the Red Sea, he waved that over the Red Sea, and the Red Sea, seeing the judgment, got scared, and moved back on either side, and made a pillar. And the marchers of Israel, walked right across, on dry land, in the bottom of the sea.
text_no:
Og slik er det i kveld. Mens vi marsjerer mot det lovede land, kaster Gud lys på veien med guddommelig helbredelse, tungetale, jubelrop og Guds herlighet. Han åpenbarer dette for lysets barn og kaster mørke tilbake slik at de andre snubler, uten å vite hvor de går.
Husk at den samme stokken, hånden som holdt domsstaven, da han kom ned og Guds vei gikk gjennom Rødehavet, vinket han over havet. Rødehavet, som så dommen, ble redd og trakk seg tilbake på begge sider, og laget en søyle. Israels marsjerende gikk tvers over, på tørt land, på havets bunn.

text:
And when the uncircumcised, trying to pretend they were something when they were nothing, started to do so, God just covered them, took the chariot wheels off and scared the horses down there in the bottom of the sea, in a great bit of conglomeration. And on the other side, that same judgment, there where sin was in the valley… Praise God. You know what led them through? That same pillar of fire that had been showing them; when it came down to this valley, it moved right over the top of that water and made a way.
Where these was back there, without the leading of the Holy Spirit, could not walk where those circumcised were walking. Hallelujah! Oh, that makes me feel religious, to think of it.
text_no:
Da de uomskårne latet som de var noe, mens de i virkeligheten var ingenting, grep Gud inn. Han fjernet hjulene fra vognene og skremte hestene ned i havets bunn i en stor forvirring. På den andre siden, i dalen der synden lå… Pris Gud. Vet du hva som ledet dem gjennom? Den samme ildsøylen som hadde vist dem veien; da de nådde denne dalen, beveget den seg over vannet og banet en vei.
De uomskårne, uten Den Hellige Ånds ledelse, kunne ikke gå der de omskårne gikk. Halleluja! Å, det får meg til å føle meg religiøs når jeg tenker på det.

text:
The fight is on. Someday we got to come down through two great big, dark valleys … or, pillars, called a valley, that's with a wall on each side; the valley of the shadows of death. David said, "I will fear no evil, for Thou art with me."
And when they seen that glorious, great light of the pillar of fire, whirling over the top of this water, seen those great big, scary hills up there, maybe forty fathoms deep, way down deep in that mucky looking stuff, at the bottom, and the Angel of the Lord leading the way. Israel knew that if the Angel was going on, it was making a way, and no fear was in the way. Amen.
Then one of these days, we've got to come down through the valley of the shadows of death, just like that was. We'll fear no evil, as long as the morning star is moving on before you, lighting up a way, and making it possible for you to walk. Just the other side is the promised land. Amen. I'm so happy for that.
Notice, then, when they got over on the other side, these uncircumcised said, "Well, if they can do it, we can too." And got drowned in the sea, confused, and drowned and sank, out in the sea.
text_no:
Kampen pågår. En dag må vi alle gå gjennom to store, mørke daler, eller søyler, som kalles skyggedalen, med en mur på hver side; Dødsskyggens dal. David sa, "Jeg frykter ikke for noe ondt, for Du er med meg."
Da de så det herlige, store lyset fra ildsøylen svirre over vannet, og de så de skremmende, store åsene der, kanskje førti favner dype, langt ned i det mørke, gjørmete bunnen, med Herrens Engel som ledet veien. Israel visste at dersom Engelen gikk foran, gjorde Den en vei, og frykt hadde ingen plass. Amen.
En dag må vi også gå gjennom dødsskyggens dal, akkurat som de gjorde. Vi vil ikke frykte noe ondt, så lenge morgenstjernen beveger seg foran oss, lyser opp veien og gjør det mulig for oss å gå. På den andre siden venter det lovede land. Amen. Jeg er så glad for det.
Legg merke til at da de kom over på den andre siden, sa de uomskårne: "Vel, hvis de kan gjøre det, kan vi også." Men de druknet i havet, forvirret, druknet og sank ut i sjøen.

text:
On they went. When they began to need water one day, God told Moses… Listen, closely. Oh, I want you to get this part of it. Here's the golden cord of it. The people were thirsting, and they were wondering, Where can we drink. Our little children are starving?
When their little children was sick, God erected a brass serpent: another symbol, another stick, like the one Moses had in his hand, only in a symbol with a snake wrapped around it. What was it? The judgment. Drawing dividends on your insurance policy. Amen. Drawing dividends on Calvary. For Moses… Jesus said, "As Moses lifted up the brass serpent in the wilderness, so must the Son of man be lifted up."
text_no:
De fortsatte videre. Da de en dag begynte å trenge vann, sa Gud til Moses… Lytt nøye. Åh, jeg vil at du skal forstå denne delen. Her er det gylne båndet. Folket var tørste og undret: Hvor kan vi drikke? Våre små barn sulter?
Da deres små barn var syke, satte Gud opp en kobberslange: et annet symbol, en annen stav, som den Moses hadde i hånden, men nå med en slange omkring seg. Hva var det? Dommen. Å få utbytte på forsikringen din. Amen. Å få utbytte på Golgata. For Moses… Jesus sa, "Slik Moses løftet opp kobberslangen i ørkenen, slik må Menneskesønnen bli løftet opp."

text:
He was lifted up for a compound reason, because they were chiding, and jostling one another, and fighting one another, and despising Moses. And they were sinning, and they were sick. And it was lifted up for two reasons: to forgive their backslidings, and to heal their sickness.
"And He was wounded for our transgressions, and with His stripes we were healed."
Scripture! Every jump of it, every word. God's eternal Word, which can never pass away. Amen. I'm so happy for that foundation that was laid by Jesus Christ, the chief cornerstone. And we're built together as blocks, in the temple of the living God, by our confession, and believing on the Lord Jesus Christ. The same life that was in them, is moving right through the building.
text_no:
Han ble løftet opp av en sammensatt grunn: de kjeftet, dyttet og sloss med hverandre, og de foraktet Moses. De syndet og var syke. Han ble løftet opp for to grunner: å tilgi deres frafall og å helbrede deres sykdom.
"Han ble såret for våre overtredelser, og ved Hans sår ble vi helbredet."
Skriften! Hvert eneste ord. Guds evige Ord, som aldri kan forgå. Amen. Jeg er så glad for det fundamentet som ble lagt av Jesus Kristus, den viktigste hjørnesteinen. Vi blir bygget sammen som blokker i tempelet til den levende Gud, gjennom vår bekjennelse og tro på Herren Jesus Kristus. Det samme livet som var i dem, beveger seg gjennom hele bygningen.

text:
Notice. Moses. Now, he said … he went out, and he prayed, he said, "Lord, these people are thirsting now, and there's no water to give them, and they're crying to me for water." And Moses and Aaron went to the tabernacle, and fell before the Lord, and the Shekinah glory of God fell around them. Amen. Shekinah glory! The presence of God come around them.
Why, brother, that's no more than what you feel in a good, old-fashioned, Holy Spirit meeting, when the Shekinah glory of God drops down among you. It's the same thing.
Moses, raising his hands before God, almost transformed before them. God said, "Moses, I'll go before you, and stand in the gap, upon the rock." I like that. "I'll go before you, and will stand upon the rock, to provide a way for you. And when you come, strike the rock. Take the rod, now, and gather the people, and go and strike this rock, and it will bring forth the waters out of the rock." Oh my! "I will stand on the rock, before you."
Look. Moses had to wait. God went before Moses and stood on the rock. Now, I want you to watch the spiritual meaning of this. When we see God's Word just unfolding, you just can bathe in it. I can, and I know you can, too.
text_no:
Legg merke til Moses nå. Han gikk ut og ba og sa, "Herre, disse menneskene tørster, og det finnes ikke vann til dem, og de roper til meg etter vann." Moses og Aron gikk til tabernaklet, falt ned for Herren, og Guds Shekinah-ære omga dem. Amen. Shekinah-ære! Guds nærvær kom over dem.
Bror, det er ikke annet enn hva du kan oppleve på et godt, gammeldags, Hellig Ånd-møte, når Guds Shekinah-ære kommer ned blant dere. Det er det samme.
Moses løftet hendene foran Gud, nærmest forvandlet foran dem. Gud sa, "Moses, Jeg skal gå foran deg og stå i gapet, på klippen." Jeg liker det. "Jeg skal gå foran deg og stå på klippen, for å gi deg en vei. Og når du kommer, slå på klippen. Ta staven og samle folket, og gå og slå på denne klippen, så den vil gi frem vann." Å, kjære! "Jeg skal stå på klippen, foran deg."
Se. Moses måtte vente. Gud gikk foran Moses og sto på klippen. Nå, jeg vil at du skal følge den åndelige betydningen av dette. Når vi ser Guds Ord utfoldes, kan vi bare dykke ned i det. Jeg kan, og jeg vet at du kan også.

text:
And there, Moses, taking the rod, then, the judgment stick, going forth to the rock, and there stood God on the rock. And he smote the rock. And when he did, the waters gushed forth. Not a little, bitty stream, like you see the artist draw the picture. I could drink that thing dry, if I was thirsty. But look. Enough water came out of it to water two million people, plus camels and animals that they had. Come forth with gushers. Hallelujah!
Beautiful type of it, when Jesus said, in the wilderness, that He was the Rock that was in the wilderness. No wonder the people screamed, back there, that day, when He came in to Jerusalem, riding on this horse … little mule. And they were crying, those Nazarenes crying, "Hosanna, hosanna, to Him that cometh in the name of the Lord."
Some of those self-styled, starchy priests said, "Make them hold their peace."
"Why," he said, "if they hold their peace, the rocks would immediately cry out."
text_no:
Moses tok staven, domsstaven, og gikk frem til klippen, hvor Gud sto. Han slo klippen, og vannet strømmet ut. Ikke en liten bekk, slik kunstnere ofte fremstiller det. Jeg kunne trolig drikke den tom om jeg var tørst. Men se, nok vann flommet ut til å slukke tørsten til to millioner mennesker, pluss kameler og andre dyr de hadde med seg. Vannstrømmene kom som flodbølger. Halleluja!
En vakker illustrasjon av dette finner vi når Jesus sa at Han var Klippen i ørkenen. Det er ikke rart at folkemengden ropte den dagen da Han kom inn i Jerusalem ridende på et lite esel. Nazareerne ropte: "Hosanna, hosanna, velsignet er Han som kommer i Herrens navn."
Noen av de selvhøytidelige, stive prestene sa: "Få dem til å tie."
Jesus svarte: "Hvis de tier, vil steinene straks rope."

text:
What is the rocks. The rocks has been washed by the same waters, watered by the Word. They have to cry out. Said, "If they hold their peace, the rocks will immediately cry out." Something had to take place. The rocks. Notice.
Later on, when they needed some water, only thing they had to do, was speak to the rock. One day they were hungry, and they went hunting, and they found in this cleft, in the rock, was honey--hanging in the cleft.
The cleft is, in symbol, was the spear mark in the side of the Lord Jesus. The cleft in the rock, where it was smitten. And in there, a bunch of bees had come and built a hive. And there was honey in the rock. Amen. What a beautiful thing.
Samson, when he was going down to see his wife, his girlfriend, and a lion run out. Little, old fellow, about that big, seven little curls hanging down, little old, sissy-like. And people couldn't understand how that fellow could be so strong. And a great big, old lion run out, and … said, "Well looky here, what's coming." Samson, just a little, old, sissy, standing there, and the lion run out, many times his size. And then, what taken place?
text_no:
Hva er steinene? Steinene har blitt vasket av de samme vannene, overrislet av Ordet. De må rope ut. Det står: "Hvis de tier, skal steinene rope." Noe måtte skje. Steinene. Legg merke til.
Senere, når de trengte vann, var det eneste de måtte gjøre å tale til steinen. En dag var de sultne og gikk på jakt. I en kløft i steinen fant de honning—hengende i kløften.
Kløften, i symbol, var spydmerket i siden på Herren Jesus. Kløften i steinen, hvor den ble slått. Der hadde en bisverm bygget en kube, og det var honning i steinen. Amen. For en vakker ting.
Da Samson skulle besøke sin kone, sin kjæreste, sprang en løve fram. Samson, en liten mann med syv små krøller som hang ned, og virket puslete. Folk kunne ikke forstå hvordan en slik mann kunne være så sterk. En stor løve sprang fram og sa: "Se her hva som kommer." Samson, bare en liten fyr, sto der, og løven, mange ganger hans størrelse, sprang frem. Hva skjedde så?

text:
Samson never walked up first, and said, "Here, I'll tear you apart." But the Spirit of the Lord come upon him. That's what did it. He felt back there, and felt these seven locks hanging down. That was the covenant. And he wasn't scared of the lion. In the face of death, he wasn't scared of it.
If you can feel back … if Samson could feel seven locks, and know it was a covenant, because he was borned a Nazarite, how much more ought a man that's borned of the Holy Spirit, feel the power of God, and know it's the covenant, to slay the enemy before you. Whether it's sickness, whether it's temptation, whatever it is, you can't stand it, when the Spirit of God comes.
text_no:
Samson gikk aldri først og sa: "Her, jeg skal rive deg i stykker." Men Herrens Ånd kom over ham. Det var det som gjorde det. Han kjente på de syv lokkene som hang ned, og visste at det var pakten. Derfor fryktet han ikke løven. Selv i møte med døden var han ikke redd.
Hvis Samson kunne kjenne de syv lokkene og vite at det var pakten, fordi han var født som en Nasireer, hvor mye mer skal ikke en som er født av Den Hellige Ånd, føle Guds kraft og vite at det er pakten, for å overvinne fienden foran seg. Enten det er sykdom, fristelser, eller hva det måtte være, kan det ikke stå seg når Guds Ånd kommer.

text:
Walked over and grabbed that lion, that little, bitty, curly-headed shrimp, grabbed that lion by the jaw, and tore it apart. Hallelujah! You only need One; that's Christ, the covenant. Can you feel it? Amen. Oh my! The covenant.
Then, when he threw that old lion, just like it was a little, bitty, old rabbit, or something. Pulled him apart, like this, and whipped his old carcass over, went walking on down through there. A few days he came by, and a bee-hive was built in it. And he eat the sweetest honey he ever eat, out of that old bee-hive that was built in the carcass of the lion that was going to kill him. For the Spirit of God come and delivered him.
text_no:
Han gikk bort og grep løven. Denne lille, krøllete figuren tok tak i løvens kjever og rev den fra hverandre. Halleluja! Du trenger bare én; det er Kristus, pakten. Kan du føle det? Amen. Å, min Gud! Pakten.
Da han kastet den gamle løven, som om den bare var en liten kanin eller noe lignende, rev han den fra hverandre og kastet det gamle skroget. Så gikk han videre. Noen dager senere kom han forbi, og et bikube hadde blitt bygget i det. Han spiste den søteste honningen han noen gang hadde smakt, fra den gamle bikuben som var bygget i løvens kadaver som hadde tenkt å drepe ham. For Guds Ånd kom og frelste ham.

text:
And if you've got something wrong tonight, feel the Spirit of God moving on you, tear the thing apart. And the first thing you know, you'll have a testimony that'll shake the shingles off the house. Amen. You're the covenant, circumcised by the Holy Spirit. Amen. Eat some sweet honey. Really good.
When He says … say, "Yes, brother, I believe in healing."
Amazing grace, how sweet the sound,
That saved a wretch like me,
I once was lost, but now I'm found,
I was blind, but now I see. Amen.
text_no:
Hvis det er noe galt i kveld, kjenn hvordan Guds Ånd beveger Seg i deg og riv det fra hverandre. Før du vet ordet av det, vil du ha et vitnesbyrd som vil riste taket av huset. Amen. Du er paktens folk, omskåret av Den Hellige Ånd. Amen. Spis noe søt honning. Virkelig godt.
Når Han sier... svar: "Ja, Bror, jeg tror på helbredelse."
For en utrolig nåde, hvor søt lyden,
Som frelste en elendig som meg,
Jeg var tapt, men nå er jeg funnet,
Jeg var blind, men nå ser jeg. Amen.

text:
When you used to have to be led around by the arms, great big glasses on, like that, and could hardly see where I was going. And now, I got a testimony. Because I realized my position in Christ Jesus. He made a covenant, and I received it, and believed Him. And today it's sweet honey to eat it. Tell others. Now, notice again, quickly. We'll get down there…
Then, when the rock was smitten… Very beautiful type. Them people were perishing and dying. And it was a type of "God so loved the world, that He gave His only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth on Him should not perish, but would have everlasting life."
text_no:
Tidligere måtte jeg bli ledet rundt ved armene, med store briller, og knapt kunne se hvor jeg gikk. Nå har jeg et vitnesbyrd fordi jeg innså min posisjon i Kristus Jesus. Han inngikk en pakt, og jeg mottok den og trodde på Ham. I dag er det som søt honning å spise den og fortelle andre om det. Nå, legg merke til dette, raskt. Vi kommer dit...
Da klippen ble slått… Et veldig vakkert bilde. Disse menneskene holdt på å omkomme og dø. Det var et bilde på "For så har Gud elsket verden at Han ga Sin enbårne Sønn, for at hver den som tror på Ham ikke skal gå fortapt, men ha evig liv."

text:
In the midst of a perishing, dying people, a perishing, dying nation, God so loved the unworthy. Look how unworthy they was. And God so loved… Oh, if it wasn't for the love of God, He'd destroy the whole world tonight. But He can't do it. His love won't let Him do it. So, "He so loved the world, that He gave His only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in Him, should not perish, but could drink the waters of life freely. Whosoever will may come."
Just enough! A bountiful blessing pouring out! "He that believeth on me…" In other words, taking it like this: He that believeth on Me shall have a great, big gusher right in the middle of his soul, bubbling up unto everlasting life. Oh, what a beautiful thing!
text_no:
Midt blant et døende folk og en døende nasjon elsket Gud de uverdige så høyt. Se hvor uverdige de var. Og Gud elsket dem så høyt… Hvis det ikke var for Guds kjærlighet, ville Han ha ødelagt hele verden i natt. Men Han kan ikke gjøre det. Hans kjærlighet hindrer Ham. Så, "Han elsket verden så høyt at Han gav Sin enbårne Sønn, for at hver den som tror på Ham, ikke skal gå fortapt, men ha evig liv. Den som vil, kan komme."
Det er nok! En overflod av velsignelser strømmer ut! "Den som tror på Meg…" Med andre ord: Den som tror på Meg, skal ha en stor, strømmende kilde midt i sjelen, som bobler opp til evig liv. Å, hvor vakkert!

text:
Notice, that God took all the judgments of death. The death that I should die, the hell that I should go to; God, with His judgments of death and sickness, Christ met Him in the gap, hanging between the heavens and earth. And God struck upon Him, the iniquity of us all.
There, the judgment was smitten. And tonight, that ensign of the cross, a blind-folder to the world, but to those who march on, with the cross going on before them, the judgments is paid.
Look and live, my brother, live.
Look to Jesus, now, and live.
For it's recorded in His Word,
hallelujah!
It's only that we look and live.
text_no:
Merk at Gud tok på Seg all dødsdom. Døden jeg skulle dø, helvetet jeg skulle gå til; Gud, med Sin dom over død og sykdom, ble møtt av Kristus som stod i gapet, hengende mellom himmel og jord. Gud la all vår synd på Ham.
Der, ble dommen fullbyrdet. I kveld, det tegnet av korset, en blindfold for verden, men for dem som marsjerer videre med korset foran seg, er dommen betalt.
Se og lev, min bror, lev.
Se på Jesus nå og lev.
For det er skrevet i Hans Ord,
halleluja!
Det er bare ved å se og leve.

text:
If you're sick, look and live. If you're down-hearted, look and live. If you're weary, look and live. If you're dead in sin and trespasses, look and live. That's all. For the cross goes on before us.
God is putting before us this ensign, the cross, realizing that through that come… Just like the serpent, it wasn't the stick; it was the serpent represented on there, that made the atonement. And it wasn't … it ain't the wooden cross that we pack, it's the Holy Spirit that's wrapped into that great sign yonder, ensign on Calvary, when the life of the Holy Spirit was in Christ, came out of Him. It went to whosoever might come from this cross. And the cross is God's sign, before us, tonight, to follow Jesus. My!
text_no:
Om du er syk, se og lev. Om du er nedtrykt, se og lev. Om du er sliten, se og lev. Om du er død i synd og overtredelser, se og lev. Det er alt. For korset går foran oss.
Gud setter dette banneret, korset, foran oss, vel vitende om at gjennom det... Akkurat som med slangen, det var ikke stokken; det var slangen som representerte soningen. Og det er ikke trekorset vi bærer, men Den Hellige Ånd som innhyller det store tegnet på Golgata. Da Den Hellige Ånds liv i Kristus forlot Ham, gikk det til hvem som helst som kom til dette korset. Korset er Guds tegn foran oss i kveld, for å følge Jesus. Min Gud!

text:
I think of God, standing there, on the rock. God was that rock and Moses' judgment rod. Judgment, God's judgment smote Christ, dying in our stead, the innocent for the guilty.
And out of Him came the blood cell. And the life-giving blood flowed from His back, that we might be healed; flowed from his heart, that we might be saved. Come down over His brows, and things, for our iniquity, for our peace, and all was upon Him. There He was a bleeding malefactor, hanging there between heavens and earth. Standing, where a Holy God, looking down, could not see the sinner no more, for He looked through that judgment that had been paid, yonder; the all supreme price. Oh, I hope you see it, my Christian friend.
text_no:
Jeg ser for meg Gud stående der på klippen. Gud var den klippen og Moses' domsstav. Guds dom rammet Kristus, som døde i vårt sted, den uskyldige for de skyldige. 
Fra Ham kom blodet, og det livgivende blodet fløt fra Hans rygg så vi kunne bli helbredet; fløt fra Hans hjerte, så vi kunne bli frelst. Det rant ned over Hans panner, for vår misgjerning, for vår fred – alt ble lagt på Ham. Der hang Han, en blødende forbryter, mellom himmel og jord. Gud, den Hellige, kunne fra sin himmel ikke lenger se synderen, fordi Han så gjennom den dommen som allerede var betalt; den høyeste pris. Åh, jeg håper du ser det, min kristne venn.

text:
And look yonder, there it is. Therefore, as God is concerned, it's finished. The price is paid. You're free. That's what's the trouble with people, tonight. They don't know they're free.
Here not long ago, an old farmer had a corn field. The crows kept coming by eating up his corn, so he set a trap and caught one. He said, "I'll fix the rest of them." He tied that by its foot, to the fence, and that poor, old crow hobbled and jumped, and hobbled and jumped, and he couldn't get away, because he was tied up. He was starving; eat everything around where he could reach. He was starving to death. He got so he just couldn't hardly fly, or jump up and down any more.
text_no:
Se der borte, der er det. For Gud er det ferdig. Prisen er betalt. Du er fri. Det er problemet med mange i kveld. De vet ikke at de er fri.
Ikke for lenge siden hadde en gammel bonde en maisåker. Kråkene kom stadig for å spise opp maisen hans, så han satte en felle og fanget en. Han sa, "Jeg skal fikse resten av dem." Han bandt den til gjerdet ved foten, og den stakkars gamle kråka humplet og hoppet, men kom seg ikke unna fordi den var bundet fast. Den sultet og spiste alt i nærheten av rekkevidde. Den var så sulten at den nesten ikke kunne fly eller hoppe opp og ned lenger.

text:
All the other crows would come by and holler, "Come on, Johnny Crow. Let's go south. It's turning winter. You'll die sitting there. Let's go, Johnny Crow."
He said, "I can't. I'm tied."
One day, a good man came by. Said, "That poor, old crow. Look at him, hobbling around there. He's getting weak." So, he caught the old fellow, petted him a little bit, took the latch off of his foot, like that, and turned him loose, and said, "All right, old boy, go on."
Walked away. And the old crow just kept walking around the same circle--same circle. See, he was untied, and didn't know it. The other crows came by and said, "Come on, Johnny Crow. Let's go south. It's coming winter."
Said, "I can't. I'm still tied."
text_no:
Alle de andre kråkene kom forbi og ropte: "Kom igjen, Johnny Kråke. La oss dra sørover. Det blir vinter. Du kommer til å dø hvis du blir sittende her. Kom igjen, Johnny Kråke."
Han svarte, "Jeg kan ikke. Jeg er bundet."
En dag kom en god mann forbi og sa: "Den stakkars gamle kråken. Se på ham, haltende rundt der. Han blir svakere." Så fanget han den gamle karen, strøk ham litt, tok av båndet fra foten hans, og slapp ham fri. "Greit, gamle venn, nå kan du gå."
Mannen gikk sin vei. Og den gamle kråken fortsatte bare å gå i samme sirkel—samme sirkel. Se, han var ubundet, men visste det ikke. De andre kråkene kom forbi og sa: "Kom igjen, Johnny Kråke. La oss dra sørover. Vinteren kommer."
Han svarte, "Jeg kan ikke. Jeg er fortsatt bundet."

text:
That's the way it is with men and women tonight, who don't know their position in Christ Jesus. The devil may have you tied with TB, may have you tied with a cancer, he may have you tied to a many thing. But Christ, the good Man, has stood your judgment yonder and set you free tonight. God bless your hearts. You can rise in the power of the Holy Ghost. Let's not go south, but let's go towards Glory, marching as the great army of the living God. We're free. Can't you hear the others saying, "Come on."
I once was bound by a chain of sin, but tonight I've been cut loose, by Jesus Christ. Amen.
text_no:
Slik er det med menn og kvinner i kveld som ikke kjenner sin posisjon i Kristus Jesus. Djevelen kan ha bundet deg med tuberkulose, kreft eller andre ting. Men Kristus, den gode Mannen, har tatt din dom der borte og satt deg fri i kveld. Gud velsigne hjertene deres. Dere kan reise dere i Den Hellige Ånds kraft. La oss ikke gå sørover, men mot Herlighet, marsjerende som den store hæren til den levende Gud. Vi er fri. Hører du ikke de andre si, "Kom igjen"?
Jeg var en gang bundet av syndens lenke, men i kveld har Jesus Kristus satt meg fri. Amen.

text:
The judgments. Sure. Christ took our judgment, the innocent for the guilty. He was made sin that we might not be sinners. He bore our sickness, that we wouldn't have to bear it. He bore our sorrows that we wouldn't have to sorrow no more, "as others, which have no hope," says the Scripture. He was … all that we need was right in Him. You believe that? Certainly.
Do you believe that for your heart trouble, lady sitting there? You believe that for that heart condition? Do you really accept it now, with all your heart? Then, He bore your heart trouble, you ain't got it no more now. Amen. If you believe it, you can have it. Hallelujah! Yes, sir.
text_no:
Dommer. Selvsagt. Kristus tok vår dom, den uskyldige for de skyldige. Han ble gjort til synd for at vi ikke skulle være syndere. Han bar våre sykdommer slik at vi ikke skulle bære dem. Han bar våre sorger slik at vi ikke skulle ha noen sorg mer, "som de andre som ikke har håp," sier Skriften. Han var ... alt vi trenger finnes i Ham. Tror dere det? Absolutt.
Tror du på det for ditt hjerteproblem, dame som sitter der? Tror du det gjelder ditt hjerte? Vil du virkelig akseptere det nå, med hele ditt hjerte? Da har Han båret ditt hjerteproblem, og du har det ikke lenger. Amen. Hvis du tror det, kan du motta det. Halleluja! Ja, sir.

text:
"He was wounded for our transgressions, bruised for our iniquity, the chastisement of our peace was upon Him, and with His stripes we were healed." After that rock was once smitten, God told Moses, said, "Go on, Moses. The only thing you have to do now, is go speak to that rock, from this on. Every time you need water, speak to it. You don't have to smite it any more. You don't have to pound all night long. You don't have to worry, and beg, and pray, and fast, and go on. Just speak to the rock." Hallelujah! "And it will bring forth His waters." Amen. "Speak to the rock, and it'll bring forth His waters."
I tell you, one time, there was a woman with a blood issue, and all that she had was lost. She could not be healed by any doctor. And she came down along the bank, one day, and she seen that spiritual rock rolling by, and she spake to the Rock. Just as this woman did here, just a few minutes ago--spoke to the Rock and out of the Rock came healing waters. Hallelujah!
text_no:
"Hans sår har lægt våre overtredelser; straffen lå på Ham for at vi skulle ha fred, og ved Hans sår har vi fått legedom." Etter at klippen én gang var slått, sa Gud til Moses: "Gå videre, Moses. Det eneste du nå trenger å gjøre, er å tale til den klippen. Hver gang du trenger vann, tal til den. Du trenger ikke slå den mer. Du trenger ikke hamre hele natten. Du trenger ikke bekymre deg, tigge, be, faste og streve. Bare tal til klippen." Halleluja! "Og den vil gi sitt vann." Amen. "Tal til klippen, og den vil gi sitt vann."
Jeg forteller deg, en gang var det en kvinne med blodsykdom som hadde mistet alt hun eide. Ingen lege kunne helbrede henne. En dag kom hun ned langs elvebredden og så den åndelige Klippen rulle forbi. Hun talte til Klippen. Akkurat som denne kvinnen gjorde for noen få minutter siden—talte til Klippen, og fra Klippen kom helbredende vann. Halleluja!

text:
There was a little, old man named Jairus, who really loved the Lord, but he was a secret worshiper. And one day, his little girl died. All hopes was gone, but he spake to the Rock, and the Rock gave not only water, but life. The waters of life, freely.
Another woman by the name of Martha, and Mary, they loved Him. And He'd gone from their home. And their brother was dead, and rottening, out there in the grave. One day, they heard that the Rock was coming, rolling in to the city. And Martha had been very dilatory about cooking good dinners for Him, and things, and not maybe paying so much attention to the spiritual. But in her heart, she believed it. For she showed, then, what she was. She run out, and knelt down on her knees, and she spoke to the Rock. And the Rock, in return, spoke in eternity, and raised a dead man out of his grave. Hallelujah!
text_no:
Det var en liten, gammel mann ved navn Jairus, som virkelig elsket Herren, men han var en hemmelig tilbeder. En dag døde hans lille datter. Alt håp var ute, men han talte til Klippen, og Klippen ga ikke bare vann, men liv. Livets vann, fritt og uten kostnad.
En annen kvinne ved navn Martha, og hennes søster Maria, elsket Ham. Han hadde forlatt deres hjem, og deres bror var død og i ferd med å råtne i graven. En dag hørte de at Klippen kom, rullende inn i byen. Martha hadde vært opptatt med å lage gode middager for Ham og andre huslige oppgaver, kanskje uten å legge så mye vekt på det åndelige. Men i sitt hjerte trodde hun. For hun viste da hvem hun virkelig var. Hun løp ut, knelte ned og talte til Klippen. Og Klippen svarte med Evigheten og vekket en død mann opp fra graven. Halleluja!

text:
When a bunch of people was out on a little old boat, one night, and it was poppering around, like a bottle stopper, jumping from place to place, all hopes that they'd ever be saved was gone. And they happened to realize that laying in the bow of the boat, was the Rock. They spoke to the Rock about their troubles, and it soothed down, and the winds folded their arms, and went to the crevices. Hallelujah! And the sea that was rocking, smoothed out, like a mother singing a lullaby to her baby. They spake to the Rock, and the Rock brought forth the substance that they asked for.
Are you on speaking terms with Him tonight? Could you speak to Him about your chest trouble, sitting there? You believe that God has healed you? You just got through speaking to Him, didn't you? Didn't you say, "Lord, heal me." You spoke to Him, and your chest trouble is gone now. "Speak to the Rock. He'll bring forth His waters." Hallelujah!
text_no:
En gruppe mennesker var ute i en liten gammel båt en natt. Båten var i voldsom bevegelse, som en vinopptrekker som hopper fra sted til sted. Alt håp om å bli reddet var borte. Da innså de at i baugen av båten lå Klippen. De talte til Klippen om sine problemer, og det roet seg. Vinden foldet sine armer og gikk til sprekkene. Halleluja! Sjøen, som hadde vært i kraftig bevegelse, jevnet seg ut som en mor som synger en vuggesang for sitt barn. De talte til Klippen, og Klippen ga dem det de bad om.
Er du på talefot med Ham i kveld? Kan du tale til Ham om dine brystproblemer der du sitter? Tror du at Gud har helbredet deg? Du har nettopp talt til Ham, har du ikke? Sa du ikke, "Herre, helbred meg"? Du talte til Ham, og nå er brystproblemet borte. "Tal til Klippen. Han vil gi Sitt vann." Halleluja!

text:
Moses, when his time had come, and everything had failed, God said, "Come up," and he climbed up to the top of Mount Nebo, to look over into the promised land. There death came whirling through the air. He knew that death was all around him. And Moses looked around, and he looked, laying there by his side, and there laid the rock.
He stepped up on the rock. He had followed it all the way through the wilderness … or, it had followed him. He stepped up on the rock, and God sent some pallbearers--Angels--down, and packed him away, off the rock. Hallelujah! That rock had fed him when he was hungry, give him water when he was thirsty, and taken him to home in glory, when he was dying.
text_no:
Da tiden var inne for Moses, og alt hadde feilet, sa Gud: "Kom opp," og han klatret til toppen av Nebo-fjellet for å se over til det lovede land. Der kom døden virvlende gjennom luften. Han visste at døden var rundt ham. Moses så seg omkring, og der, ved hans side, lå en stein.
Han steg opp på steinen. Han hadde fulgt den gjennom hele ørkenen … eller, den hadde fulgt ham. Han steg opp på steinen, og Gud sendte noen bærere—Engler—ned for å hente ham fra steinen. Halleluja! Den steinen hadde gitt ham mat når han var sulten, gitt ham vann når han var tørst, og tatt ham hjem i herlighet, når han var døende.

text:
My prayer is tonight, "Lord Jesus, pull me up above this worldly element here, till I can be on speaking terms with You. That someday, when I go down through the valley of the shadow of death, I expect to see the Rock standing there." Hallelujah! "Keep me on speaking terms with Him. I, myself, want to speak to Him."
You need to speak to Him. He's here, tonight, and He's on speaking terms with every one of you, if you want to speak to Him. Do you believe it? For whatever need you have need of, let us stand to our feet at this time, now. Oh my!
Oh, I feel like if you'd put this church on my shoulders, I could walk away with it. What? The Rock, Christ Jesus is here.
text_no:
Min bønn i kveld er: "Herre Jesus, løft meg opp over dette verdslige elementet, slik at jeg kan være i samtale med Deg. At når jeg en dag går gjennom dødsskyggens dal, forventer jeg å se Klippen stå der." Halleluja! "Hold meg på talefot med Ham. Jeg ønsker selv å tale med Ham."
Du trenger å tale med Ham. Han er her i kveld, og Han er på talefot med hver og en av dere, hvis dere ønsker å tale med Ham. Tror dere det? For hvilken som helst behov dere har, la oss reise oss nå. Å, min Gud!
Å, jeg føler at dersom dere plasserte denne menigheten på mine skuldre, kunne jeg bære den bort. Hvorfor? Klippen, Kristus Jesus, er her.

text:
Oh, I'm not excited. I know exactly where I'm standing. I know exactly what I'm talking about. Oh, if I could only express my feelings. If I could only tell this audience what it really means to be sitting under the anointing of this Rock, Christ Jesus. Already paid your judgment; bore your sickness; took your sorrows, and settled the question before God. Are you scared to put your trust in Him?
You don't have to be judged. You don't have to think yourself worthy; you'll never be worthy. But Christ was worthy in your stead. That paid the price. Not trying to redeem something. You can't redeem it, 'cause you ain't got the money to. But God had the money, and He sent Christ, and settled the bill, and redeemed you from your sickness, and your sorrows, and your worry, and your sin, and your backsliding. He sent the money, which was Christ Jesus, and deposited it on Calvary, on Golgotha's hill. And tonight, every one of you is free, if you'll just believe it.

Jeg er ikke oppspilt. Jeg vet nøyaktig hvor jeg står. Jeg vet nøyaktig hva jeg snakker om. Åh, om jeg bare kunne uttrykke mine følelser. Om jeg bare kunne fortelle dette publikum hva det virkelig betyr å sitte under salvelsen av denne Klippen, Kristus Jesus. Han har allerede betalt din dom, båret din sykdom, tatt dine sorger og avgjort saken overfor Gud. Er du redd for å sette din lit til Ham?
Du trenger ikke å bli dømt. Du trenger ikke å tenke at du er verdig; du vil aldri bli verdig. Men Kristus var verdig i ditt sted. Han betalte prisen. Du kan ikke løskjøpe noe, for du har ikke midlene. Men Gud hadde midlene, og Han sendte Kristus som gjorde opp regningen og løskjøpte deg fra din sykdom, dine sorger, din bekymring, din synd og din frafall. Han sendte midlene, som var Kristus Jesus, og deponerte dem på Golgata. Og i kveld er hver og en av dere fri, hvis dere bare vil tro det.

text:
O Christ of God, I speak to You, as Moses of old, in behalf of this perishing people with sickness and with troubles. O everlasting God, from the everlasting hills, lift up Thine head, Lord, and receive this people into Thy kingdom, and, Lord, may the Holy Ghost so anoint these people tonight, that they'll feel their covenant. Like Samson did, when he felt the covenant hair. May the Holy Ghost wrap around every heart tonight, till the people'll realize that it's the covenant that You made with them, and they can go and be free.
I condemn every sickness, every disease, every sin. I say, as Your servant, Lord, in the word of humility to You, but in a bitter rebuke before the devil, "Satan, you've lost the battle. You might as well give it up. In the name of Jesus Christ, come out of every person in here, that they can be made free." Amen.
text_no:
O Kristus fra Gud, jeg taler til Deg, slik Moses gjorde i gamle dager, på vegne av dette fortapte folket som lider av sykdom og problemer. O evige Gud, fra de evige åser, løft Ditt hode, Herre, og ta imot dette folket i Ditt rike. Herre, må Den Hellige Ånd salve disse menneskene i kveld, så de føler sitt paktforhold. Som Samson gjorde, da han følte paktens hår. Må Den Hellige Ånd omfavne hvert hjerte i kveld, slik at folket innser at det er pakten Du har inngått med dem, og at de kan gå og være fri.
Jeg fordømmer hver sykdom, hver lidelse, hver synd. Jeg sier, som Din tjener, Herre, i ydmykhetens ord til Deg, men i en bitter irettesettelse foran djevelen: "Satan, du har tapt kampen. Du kan like godt gi opp. I Jesu Kristi navn, kom ut av hver person her inne, så de kan bli frigjort." Amen.
